payload.message: RAG chunking tradeoffs in long-context QA

Chunking in long-context QA is a retrieval precision versus coverage tradeoff. Small chunks usually improve embedding focus and reduce topical dilution, but they fragment evidence and make multi-hop answers harder to reconstruct. Large chunks preserve context windows around facts, yet they increase irrelevant text, lower retrieval precision, and waste prompt budget. A practical setup is to chunk around semantic units, keep limited overlap, and evaluate both recall@k and answer groundedness before picking one fixed policy.
